signals.title: Private label pressure remains active in French grocery
signals.summary: Retailer affordability language is still the strongest buyer anchor in France.
signals.negotiationImplication: Buyers may use private label shelf gaps to resist PepsiCo price realization.
signals.recommendedAction: Model downside realization for Carrefour and validate shelf price proof.

caveats: Prototype public signal, not live web research.
governance.replacementRequirement: Linked public source or approved market intelligence feed

signals.title: France regulatory pricing scrutiny raises buyer sensitivity
signals.summary: Negotiation teams should expect more pressure on price justification and customer-safe proof.
signals.negotiationImplication: Cost proof and corridor rationale should be source-backed before buyer use.
signals.recommendedAction: Retrieve approved pricing corridor and validate current cost pressure stack.

caveats: Prototype public signal, not legal advice.
governance.replacementRequirement: Linked public source or approved legal/regulatory summary

signals.title: German retailers continue margin-pressure messaging
signals.summary: German buying groups are expected to press for margin relief and higher promo support.
signals.negotiationImplication: Expected realization is under plan; downside scenario modeling should be prioritized.
signals.recommendedAction: Model EDEKA downside realization and Rewe promo exposure.

signals.title: Private label growth strengthens discount buyer leverage
signals.summary: Value-tier and private label momentum may increase resistance to branded price moves.
signals.negotiationImplication: Buyer leverage increases where value-pack and private-label comparisons are weak.
signals.recommendedAction: Review competitor value-pack evidence before EDEKA and Aldi pre-reads.

signals.title: UK cost-of-living sensitivity keeps promo expectations high
signals.summary: UK grocery buyers may press for value packs and promotion depth before accepting price realization.
signals.negotiationImplication: Trade spend exposure should be modeled alongside price realization.
signals.recommendedAction: Model Tesco value-pack pressure with trade spend sensitivity.

governance.replacementRequirement: Approved economic or market intelligence feed

signals.title: Benelux retailer consolidation increases cross-market corridor pressure
signals.summary: Cross-border buying logic may pressure Netherlands and Belgium price corridors.
signals.negotiationImplication: Teams should compare corridors across markets before customer-facing claims.
signals.recommendedAction: Retrieve cross-market pricing corridor and save validation task to timeline.

competitorMoves.title: French private label price gaps widen in salty snacks
competitorMoves.summary: Private label shelf price gap is being used as buyer leverage against branded suppliers.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer can argue branded price moves are out of step with affordability pressure.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: PepsiCo needs value and category-growth proof, not only cost pressure proof.
competitorMoves.recommendedAction: Model Carrefour expected realization at 2.4% and attach shelf-price source caveats.

caveats: Validate public source before pilot use.

competitorMoves.title: Value-pack messaging increases in Western Europe
competitorMoves.summary: Public value-pack and affordability messages may reset buyer expectations for branded snacks.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer can request PepsiCo matching value-pack or promo support.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: Scenario should include volume upside and trade spend sensitivity together.
competitorMoves.recommendedAction: Create value-pack pressure scenario for Tesco and Carrefour.

caveats: Validate public source before pilot use.

competitorMoves.title: German promotional intensity increases in snacks aisle
competitorMoves.summary: Higher promotional depth creates buyer leverage around event support and trade spend.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer can point to competitive promo depth as a reason to resist base price moves.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: Margin-at-risk and trade spend exposure should be modeled before concessions.
competitorMoves.recommendedAction: Model EDEKA trade-spend downside and Rewe promo exposure.

caveats: Validate public source before pilot use.

competitorMoves.title: UK value-pack activity reinforces affordability framing
competitorMoves.summary: Public pack architecture moves in adjacent categories may influence grocery buyer expectations.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer may benchmark across categories and ask for PepsiCo affordability mechanics.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: Scenario should show price realization and volume effects together.
competitorMoves.recommendedAction: Model Tesco value-pack pressure with accepted realization sensitivity.

competitorMoves.title: Sustainability claims increase in Benelux customer materials
competitorMoves.summary: Sustainability messaging can become a negotiating proof point for customer value.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer may ask PepsiCo to match proof beyond price and promo mechanics.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: Document retrieval should check for approved customer-safe proof before AI generates claims.
competitorMoves.recommendedAction: Retrieve approved Benelux customer profile before creating new content.

caveats: Validate public source before pilot use.

competitorMoves.title: German private label value tier continues expansion
competitorMoves.summary: Private label value-tier activity raises buyer leverage in discount and full-line retail.
competitorMoves.possibleBuyerLeverage: Buyer may use value-tier growth to challenge PepsiCo price corridor.
competitorMoves.pepsicoImplication: Price realization and volume downside should be modeled for EDEKA first.

caveats: Validate public source before pilot use.

timelineEvents.title: Carrefour risk remains high
timelineEvents.summary: Expected realization is 2.4% versus 3.5% target; fallback scenario still needs validation.

timelineEvents.title: ATLAS retrieved approved Carrefour debrief
timelineEvents.summary: Using the 2025 Carrefour Debrief before generating new customer memory.

timelineEvents.title: Carrefour 3.2% realization scenario modeled
timelineEvents.summary: Scenario improves revenue but increases buyer acceptance risk and requires validation.

timelineEvents.title: Debrief saved to Carrefour memory
timelineEvents.summary: ATLAS generated a post-meeting debrief with buyer ask, pricing guardrail pressure, promo exposure and Germany offset validation needs.

caveats: Human validation required before official use.

timelineEvents.title: EDEKA gap to plan increased
timelineEvents.summary: Expected realization remains 2.0% against 3.2% target, making Germany the top exposure.

timelineEvents.title: Kellanova promotion pressure linked to EDEKA
timelineEvents.summary: Competitor promo depth may increase EDEKA buyer leverage.

timelineEvents.title: Round 3 counter shifted pressure to trade funding
timelineEvents.summary: EDEKA held its 1.8% landing position and requested €640K in incremental promotional support before accepting the Germany price move.

timelineEvents.title: Finance validated 2.8% floor before escalation
timelineEvents.summary: Finance/NRM confirmed that concessions below 2.8% realization would breach the Germany corridor unless offset by volume commitment or lower trade funding.

timelineEvents.title: Opening ask set at 3.6% with phased service value
timelineEvents.summary: The CNO team opened above the 3.2% target to leave room for a phased landing path tied to assortment discipline and execution support.

timelineEvents.title: 2025 final agreement landed after four rounds
timelineEvents.summary: EDEKA accepted a 2.6% phased increase after PepsiCo held the base price and traded only time-bound execution support.

timelineEvents.title: 2025 counter pattern: early affordability pushback
timelineEvents.summary: Buyer challenged the opening ask with affordability language in rounds 1-2, then moved to competitor benchmarking once price proof improved.

timelineEvents.title: 2025 debrief: evidence worked when tied to category value
timelineEvents.summary: Debrief notes show EDEKA responded better to category-growth proof and service-value framing than to broad commodity-cost claims.

timelineEvents.title: UK affordability signal linked to Tesco
timelineEvents.summary: Promo and value-pack pressure should be modeled before Tesco pre-read.

timelineEvents.title: Round 7 buyer ask moved to value-pack funding
timelineEvents.summary: Tesco held the base-price challenge and shifted pressure toward value-pack funding and temporary promo support.

timelineEvents.title: Value-pack concession scenario saved
timelineEvents.summary: ATLAS modeled a limited value-pack concession that protects the red line but raises trade-spend exposure.

timelineEvents.title: 2025 debrief: Tesco conceded when proof was SKU-level enough
timelineEvents.summary: Prior-year debrief shows Tesco accepted price movement when PepsiCo paired the ask with shopper-value evidence and category margin proof.

timelineEvents.title: 2024 decision: hold base price, trade on promo windows
timelineEvents.summary: The team preserved base price and used time-bound promo windows as the concession path; this remains the strongest historical playbook.

timelineEvents.title: Ahold Delhaize profile available
timelineEvents.summary: Approved customer profile can be reused before generating Benelux content.

timelineEvents.title: Rewe prep moved to high risk
timelineEvents.summary: Promo depth pressure increases margin-at-risk before formal buyer update.

timelineEvents.title: Aldi private-label watch opened
timelineEvents.summary: Discount value-tier activity is being monitored for Germany and Poland.

timelineEvents.title: Lidl value-tier monitor updated
timelineEvents.summary: Monitoring remains medium-risk across Germany, UK, Netherlands and Poland.

timelineEvents.title: Auchan has no approved source
timelineEvents.summary: ATLAS would generate a draft from available signals unless an approved customer profile is uploaded.

timelineEvents.title: Intermarche private-label watch opened
timelineEvents.summary: French private-label pressure applies but financial exposure is lower than Carrefour.

timelineEvents.title: Coop outcome saved
timelineEvents.summary: Accepted realization of 2.2% saved as Italian market memory.

outputs.recommendation: Use only if finance validates Germany volume recovery and promo exposure.
Model mitigation before EDEKA governance checkpoint.
Pressure-test trade spend before committing value-pack support.

crossMarketPatterns.title: Expected realization is below target in the three highest-exposure markets
crossMarketPatterns.summary: Germany, UK and France all show expected price realization below target, with EDEKA, Tesco and Carrefour driving the gap.
crossMarketPatterns.recommendedAction: Model downside realization scenarios for EDEKA, Tesco and Carrefour before the next governance checkpoint.

caveats: Pattern is derived from synthetic financial exposure rows.
Validate against real market finance source before pilot use.
governance.replacementRequirement: Production pattern detector over approved market and buying group feeds

crossMarketPatterns.title: Private label pressure repeats across France and Germany
crossMarketPatterns.summary: Private label and value-tier pressure is visible across Carrefour, EDEKA, Aldi and Lidl, increasing buyer leverage against branded price moves.
crossMarketPatterns.recommendedAction: Attach source-backed shelf and value proof before creating customer-safe outputs.

source.sourceName: ATLAS signal and competitor pattern join

caveats: Pattern is derived from synthetic signal and competitor records.
Validate with approved external source feed.

crossMarketPatterns.title: Several active negotiations rely on stale or missing documents
crossMarketPatterns.summary: Tesco, Auchan and Intermarche have stale or missing customer intelligence, while Carrefour and EDEKA have documents that still need validation.
crossMarketPatterns.recommendedAction: Prioritize document validation before generating new CNO or customer-facing materials.

caveats: Document status is from synthetic library records.
Production should inspect actual document metadata.
governance.replacementRequirement: Document store metadata and source-owner validation workflow

cnoWatchlist.title: EDEKA is the highest financial exposure
cnoWatchlist.whyItMatters: Germany has €42M under negotiation and EDEKA contributes €7.2M margin at risk.
cnoWatchlist.recommendedAction: Open EDEKA and model downside price realization.

source.sourceName: ATLAS Europe CNO synthetic financial packet

cnoWatchlist.title: Carrefour fallback needs validation
cnoWatchlist.whyItMatters: The 3.2% fallback may help price realization, but source status is not ready for official use.
cnoWatchlist.recommendedAction: Model Carrefour 3.2% and review the validation gaps.

cnoWatchlist.title: Tesco profile is stale before UK pre-read
cnoWatchlist.whyItMatters: The active UK pressure story has shifted toward affordability and value packs since the profile was last updated.
cnoWatchlist.recommendedAction: Open Documents and validate or replace the Tesco customer profile.

cnoWatchlist.title: Private label pressure is repeating across France and Germany
cnoWatchlist.whyItMatters: This gives buyers a common affordability argument against branded price realization.
cnoWatchlist.recommendedAction: Review competitor intelligence and model the pressure in affected buying groups.
